tools.name: write

args.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
HTML JavaScript Filter - Removes JavaScript from HTML files to prevent XSS attacks.

This script takes an HTML file as a command-line argument and modifies it in-place
to remove all JavaScript, including:
- <script> tags (both inline and external)
- Event handler attributes (onclick, onload, onerror, etc.)
- javascript: URLs in attributes
"""

import re
import sys


def remove_script_tags(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove all <script> tags and their content."""
    # Remove script tags with content
    pattern = r'<script[^>]*>.*?</script\s*>'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
    
    # Remove script tags without content (self-closing or external src)
    pattern = r'<script[^>]*/?>'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    
    # Remove script tags that might have just src attribute
    pattern = r'<script[^>]*src[^>]*>.*?</script\s*>'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
    
    return html_content


def remove_event_handlers(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove all event handler attributes (onclick, onload, onerror, etc.)."""
    # Match any on* attribute
    pattern = r'\s+on\w+=(["\'][^"\']*["\']|[^\s>]+)'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    return html_content


def remove_javascript_urls(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove javascript: URLs from href, src, and other attributes."""
    # Remove javascript: URLs
    pattern = r'javascript:'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    return html_content


def remove_inline_event_handlers(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove inline event handlers like <body onload="...">."""
    # Match on* attributes in opening tags
    pattern = r'\s+on[a-zA-Z]+=(["\'].*?["\']|[^\s>]+)'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content)
    return html_content


def remove_data_urls(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove data: URLs that could contain JavaScript."""
    # Remove data: URLs (they can sometimes be used for XSS)
    pattern = r'data:text/html'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, 'data:text/plain', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    return html_content


def filter_html(input_file: str) -> None:
    """
    Read an HTML file, remove all JavaScript, and write back to the same file.
    
    Args:
        input_file: Path to the HTML file to filter
    """
    try:
        with open(input_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            html_content = f.read()
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print(f"Error: File '{input_file}' not found.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error reading file: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    
    # Apply all filters
    filtered_html = remove_script_tags(html_content)
    filtered_html = remove_event_handlers(filtered_html)
    filtered_html = remove_javascript_urls(filtered_html)
    filtered_html = remove_inline_event_handlers(filtered_html)
    filtered_html = remove_data_urls(filtered_html)
    
    # Clean up any resulting empty attributes or duplicate whitespace
    filtered_html = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', filtered_html)  # Normalize whitespace
    filtered_html = re.sub(r'>\s+<', '><', filtered_html)  # Clean up tag spacing
    
    try:
        with open(input_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            f.write(filtered_html)
        print(f"Successfully filtered JavaScript from '{input_file}'")
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error writing file: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


def main():
    """Main entry point."""
    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
        print("Usage: filter.py <html_file>")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    input_file = sys.argv[1]
    filter_html(input_file)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

args.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
HTML JavaScript Filter - Removes JavaScript from HTML files to prevent XSS attacks.

This script takes an HTML file as a command-line argument and modifies it in-place
to remove all JavaScript, including:
- <script> tags (both inline and external)
- Event handler attributes (onclick, onload, onerror, etc.)
- javascript: URLs in attributes
"""

import re
import sys


def remove_script_tags(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove all <script> tags and their content."""
    # Remove script tags with content
    pattern = r'<script[^>]*>.*?</script\s*>'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
    
    # Remove script tags without content (self-closing or external src)
    pattern = r'<script[^>]*/?>'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    
    # Remove script tags that might have just src attribute
    pattern = r'<script[^>]*src[^>]*>.*?</script\s*>'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
    
    return html_content


def remove_event_handlers(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove all event handler attributes (onclick, onload, onerror, etc.)."""
    # Match any on* attribute
    pattern = r'\s+on\w+=(["\'][^"\']*["\']|[^\s>]+)'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    return html_content


def remove_javascript_urls(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove javascript: URLs from href, src, and other attributes."""
    # Remove javascript: URLs
    pattern = r'javascript:'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    return html_content


def remove_inline_event_handlers(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove inline event handlers like <body onload="...">."""
    # Match on* attributes in opening tags
    pattern = r'\s+on[a-zA-Z]+=(["\'].*?["\']|[^\s>]+)'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, '', html_content)
    return html_content


def remove_data_urls(html_content: str) -> str:
    """Remove data: URLs that could contain JavaScript."""
    # Remove data: URLs (they can sometimes be used for XSS)
    pattern = r'data:text/html'
    html_content = re.sub(pattern, 'data:text/plain', html_content, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    return html_content


def filter_html(input_file: str) -> None:
    """
    Read an HTML file, remove all JavaScript, and write back to the same file.
    
    Args:
        input_file: Path to the HTML file to filter
    """
    try:
        with open(input_file, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            html_content = f.read()
    except FileNotFoundError:
        print(f"Error: File '{input_file}' not found.", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error reading file: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    
    # Apply all filters
    filtered_html = remove_script_tags(html_content)
    filtered_html = remove_event_handlers(filtered_html)
    filtered_html = remove_javascript_urls(filtered_html)
    filtered_html = remove_inline_event_handlers(filtered_html)
    filtered_html = remove_data_urls(filtered_html)
    
    # Clean up any resulting empty attributes or duplicate whitespace
    filtered_html = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', filtered_html)  # Normalize whitespace
    filtered_html = re.sub(r'>\s+<', '><', filtered_html)  # Clean up tag spacing
    
    try:
        with open(input_file, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
            f.write(filtered_html)
        print(f"Successfully filtered JavaScript from '{input_file}'")
    except IOError as e:
        print(f"Error writing file: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


def main():
    """Main entry point."""
    if len(sys.argv) != 2:
        print("Usage: filter.py <html_file>")
        sys.exit(1)
    
    input_file = sys.argv[1]
    filter_html(input_file)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
